title: Lithocarpus obscurus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus obscurus belongs to the family Fagaceae. This family is commonly known as the beech family. Lithocarpus obscurus is further classified under the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen trees. The species Lithocarpus obscurus is native to certain regions, and its classification also includes the kingdom Plantae. At the highest taxonomic level, Lithocarpus obscurus is classified under the domain Eukarya.
uses: Lithocarpus obscurus is a plant species with reported medicinal uses in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. The plant is said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are used to treat various ailments. The leaves and bark of the plant are used in traditional remedies to alleviate fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which may help in wound healing and skin infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Lithocarpus obscurus.

side_effects: Lithocarpus obscurus, being a plant species, does not have direct side effects on humans as it is not commonly used as medicine or consumed as food. intro: Lithocarpus obscurus, commonly known as the Himalayan Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree or shrub belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus obscurus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus obscurus is often prepared as a decoction or a powdered extract, and is commonly used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.
